_comment: Known-valid test vectors for checksum algorithms. Add vectors when implementing new generators.

cusip.rule: payload[8]: double value at 0-indexed ODD positions (i%2==1), digit-sum, (10-total%10)%10

isin.rule: 12 chars: CC(2)+NSIN(9)+check(1); Luhn on expanded numeric string

luhn.rule: double every other digit from right; digit-sum; divisible by 10

creditor_ref.standard: ISO 11649:2009 SEPA Creditor Reference (Structured Creditor Reference)
creditor_ref.rule: RF + 2-digit check + 3-21 char reference; reorder ref+RF+check → numeric; mod 97 == 1
creditor_ref.notes: RF18539007547034 from SWIFT SEPA Credit Transfer Implementation Guide

imo_number.standard: IMO Ship Identification Number (IMO Resolution A.600(15))
imo_number.rule: 7 digits: sum(d[i] * (7-i) for i in 0..5); check digit = sum % 10 == d[6]

sedol.standard: LSE SEDOL (Stock Exchange Daily Official List) 7-character identifier

nmea_gpgga.standard: NMEA 0183 GPGGA sentence (Global Positioning Fix Data)
nmea_gpgga.rule: XOR of all ASCII bytes between '$' and '*' (exclusive) == 2-hex-digit checksum after '*'

nmea_gprmc.standard: NMEA 0183 GPRMC sentence (Recommended Minimum Navigation Information)
nmea_gprmc.rule: XOR of all ASCII bytes between '$' and '*' (exclusive) == 2-hex-digit checksum after '*'

tle_satellite.standard: NORAD Two-Line Element Set (TLE) checksum (Celestrak format documentation)
tle_satellite.rule: positions 0-67: sum digits (0-9) + 1 per '-'; all other chars = 0; check = sum % 10 at position 68
tle_satellite.notes: ISS TLE from Celestrak format documentation page. Line 1 sum=157→7; Line 2 sum=197→7

gs1_128.standard: GS1-128 GTIN-14 check digit (GS1 General Specifications section 5.2)
gs1_128.rule: 14-digit GTIN: alternate weights 3,1 from position 0 for first 13 digits; check=(10-sum%10)%10

iata_ticket.standard: IATA Electronically Ticketed Document (ETD) Resolution 722 — ETN Mod-7 check digit
iata_ticket.rule: 13-digit ETN: airline_code(3) + form_serial(9) + check(1); check = int(form_serial) % 7

ir_nec.rule: 32-bit (8 hex chars): byte[0]=address, byte[1]=~address, byte[2]=command, byte[3]=~command; byte[0]^byte[1]==0xFF and byte[2]^byte[3]==0xFF

ir_rc5.standard: Philips RC-5 Infrared Protocol (36kHz carrier, 14-bit Manchester frame)
ir_rc5.rule: 14-bit word (as 16-bit): bit 13 (0x2000) = S1 start bit must always be 1; bits 12-8 = system(5), bits 5-0 = command(6)

pin_block.standard: ISO 9564-1:2017 Personal Identification Number — PIN Block Format 0
pin_block.rule: 16 hex nibbles: nibble[0]=0 (format), nibble[1]=PIN_len(4-12), nibbles[2..1+PIN_len]=PIN digits(0-9), remaining=0xF
pin_block.notes: Format 0 filler is 0xF; Format 3 filler is random 0-9; len=1 invalid (too short)

pin_block_fmt3.standard: ISO 9564-1:2017 Personal Identification Number — PIN Block Format 3
pin_block_fmt3.rule: 16 hex nibbles: nibble[0]=3 (format), nibble[1]=PIN_len(4-12), nibbles[2..1+PIN_len]=PIN digits(0-9), remaining=random 0-9 (not 0xF)
pin_block_fmt3.notes: Format 3 differs from Format 0 in format nibble (3 vs 0) and filler (0-9 vs 0xF)

ndef_uri.standard: NFC Forum Type RTD URI Record v1.0
ndef_uri.rule: Header: 0xD1 (MB|ME|SR|TNF=0x01), Type_Len=0x01, Payload_Len, Type=0x55('U'), Prefix_Code(0x00-0x23), URI_bytes

ndef_text.standard: NFC Forum Type RTD Text Record v1.0
ndef_text.rule: Header: 0xD1, Type_Len=0x01, Payload_Len, Type=0x54('T'), Status_byte, Lang_bytes(status&0x3F bytes), Text_bytes

apdu.standard: ISO/IEC 7816-4 APDU (Application Protocol Data Unit) — SELECT by AID command
apdu.rule: SELECT AID: CLA=0x00, INS=0xA4, P1=0x04 (by name), P2=0x00, Lc=AID_len, Data=AID; EMVCo RID prefix A0000000
apdu.notes: INS=0xA4 (SELECT); P1=0x04 (select by name); AID A0000000031010=Visa Credit; A0000000041010=MC Credit

epc.standard: GS1 EPC SGTIN-96 Tag Data Standard v2.0 (96-bit binary RFID encoding)
epc.rule: 96-bit (24 hex chars): byte[0]=0x30 (SGTIN-96 header); bits 8-10=filter(3), bits 11-13=partition(0-6), then company+item+serial fields
epc.notes: Header 0x30=SGTIN-96; invalid header 0xA0 and 0x10 rejected; bits 8-10 filter, bits 11-13 partition

rfid_uid.standard: ISO/IEC 14443-3A RFID UID — 7-byte format (Type A Anticollision)
rfid_uid.rule: 7 bytes (14 hex chars): byte[0] = manufacturer byte (0x04=NXP MIFARE, 0x08=Infineon, 0x02=STM); bytes 1-6 = CSPRNG serial
rfid_uid.notes: 14443-3A 7-byte UID: manufacturer byte 0x04=NXP is most common; 4-byte UIDs (8 hex) also valid per standard but generator uses 7-byte

nfc_uid.standard: NFC Forum Digital Protocol — Tag Type 2 UID (ISO 14443-3A 7-byte)
nfc_uid.rule: 7 bytes (14 hex chars): same format as rfid_uid; NFC Forum uses ISO 14443-3A UIDs for Type 2 tags
nfc_uid.notes: NFC Type 2 (MIFARE Ultralight, NTAG) uses 7-byte ISO 14443-3A UIDs; same validation as rfid_uid

track1_data.standard: ISO/IEC 7813 Financial Transaction Cards — Track 1 magnetic stripe data
track1_data.rule: Format: %B{PAN}^{NAME}^{YYMM}{SC}{DD}{DISC}? ; max 79 chars total; starts with %B, name separator ^, expiry YYMM, service code 3 digits
track1_data.notes: Starts with %B (format code B); fields separated by ^; trailing ?

track2_data.standard: ISO/IEC 7813 Financial Transaction Cards — Track 2 magnetic stripe data
track2_data.rule: Format: ;{PAN}={YYMM}{SC}{DD}? ; starts with ;, field separator =, expiry YYMM, service code 3 digits, trailing ?
track2_data.notes: Starts with ; (sentinel); field separator = (not ^); max 40 chars total

chip_data.standard: EMVCo Book 3 Application Specification — EMV TLV (Tag-Length-Value) data objects
chip_data.rule: TLV encoding: Tag 9F02 (Amount Authorized 6 bytes), 9F03 (Cashback 6 bytes), 95 (TVR 5 bytes), 5F2A (Currency 2 bytes), 9A (Date 3 bytes), 9C (Type 1 byte)
chip_data.notes: Currency 0x0949=TRY, 0x0978=EUR, 0x0826=GBP, 0x0840=USD; TVR all zeros = no issues detected

dicom_uid.standard: DICOM PS 3.5 — Unique Identifiers (UID) using 2.25 UUID-derived root
dicom_uid.rule: Format: 2.25.<decimal_uuid_integer>; max 64 chars; only digits and dots; each component non-empty; no leading zeros in components (except 2 and 25)
dicom_uid.notes: Root 2.25 = DICOM-assigned for UUID-derived UIDs; decimal integer after 2.25 represents UUID as big integer

sepa_ref.standard: ISO 20022 Payment Reference — SEPA Credit Transfer End-to-End Identification
sepa_ref.rule: 1-35 uppercase alphanumeric characters [A-Z0-9]; no spaces; SEPA core constraint: only A-Z, 0-9, space, +, -, ?, :, (, ), ., , (no lowercase)
sepa_ref.notes: SEPA End-to-End reference used in pain.001 and pacs.008; max 35 chars; uppercase only

totp_code.standard: RFC 6238 TOTP (Time-Based One-Time Password) — 6-digit numeric output
totp_code.rule: 6 decimal digits [0-9]{6}; range 000000-999999; output is HOTP(K, T) truncated to 6 digits mod 10^6
totp_code.notes: RFC 6238 Appendix B test vectors (SHA1, secret=12345678901234567890): T=1 → 287082, T=35 → 081804

webhook_signature.standard: HMAC-SHA256 Webhook Signature (GitHub, Stripe, Shopify pattern)
webhook_signature.rule: Format: sha256=<64 lowercase hex chars>; total 71 chars; hex digest is HMAC-SHA256 of payload with secret
webhook_signature.notes: Prefix 'sha256=' + 64 lowercase hex chars = 71 total chars; produced by HMAC-SHA256(secret, payload)

swift_mt103.standard: SWIFT MT103 Single Customer Credit Transfer
swift_mt103.rule:
Block structure: {1:F01...}{2:I103...}{4:
:20:
:23B:
:32A:
:50K:
:59:
:71A:
-}; Block 4 fields must include :20: :23B: :32A: :50K: :59:
swift_mt103.notes: Block 1: basic header; Block 2: application header I=input; Block 4: text block with field tags; :23B: always CRED for standard payment

emv_arqc.standard: EMVCo Book 3 — Application Cryptogram (ARQC) EMV Tag 9F26
emv_arqc.rule: 8 bytes (16 hex chars); pseudorandom but cryptographically derived from ICC Dynamic Number + UN + transaction data using 3DES
emv_arqc.notes: ARQC is 8 bytes (64-bit 3DES MAC); in practice any 16 uppercase hex chars is structurally valid for testing

emv_atc.standard: EMVCo Book 3 — Application Transaction Counter (ATC) EMV Tag 9F36
emv_atc.rule: 2 bytes (4 hex chars); 16-bit big-endian counter; range 0001-9999 for active cards; 0000 = not started
emv_atc.notes: ATC increments on every transaction; 4 hex chars (0000-FFFF); in practice 0001-270F (1-9999) for real cards

emv_iad.standard: EMVCo Issuer Application Data (IAD) EMV Tag 9F10 — Visa CVN format
emv_iad.rule: 11 bytes (22 hex chars); format 0A + DKI(1B) + CVN(1B) + CVR(2B) + ADD(4B) + PAD(2B); starts with 0A
emv_iad.notes: IAD starts with 0A (length byte=10); DKI=derived key index; CVN=cryptogram version number; all zeros in ADD/PAD is valid for testing

iso8583_auth_request.standard: ISO 8583:1987 Financial Transaction Message — MTI 0100 Authorization Request
iso8583_auth_request.rule: 4-digit MTI: '0100'; bitmap (64-bit primary as 16 hex chars) encodes present data elements; DE2=PAN, DE3=proc code, DE4=amount, DE11=STAN, DE49=currency
iso8583_auth_request.bitmap_rule: bit N set in 64-bit bitmap → Data Element N is present; primary bitmap DE1-64, secondary DE65-128 if bit 1 set
iso8583_auth_request.notes: MTI 0100=auth request, 0110=auth response, 0400=reversal request; bitmap is big-endian 64-bit; DE49 is n3 (3-digit numeric)

fedwire.standard: Fedwire Funds Service — Tag-Value message format
fedwire.rule: Tags: {1500}=Type, {2000}=Amount, {3100}=Sender DI, {3400}=Receiver DI, {1510}=Business Function; {2000} format: 12 digits (leading zeros), amount in cents
fedwire.notes: Fedwire uses {} tag delimiters; {2000}000000001000 = $10.00; amount in cents, 12 digits

edifact_orders.standard: UN/EDIFACT D96A ORDERS message — UNH/UNT segment count validation
edifact_orders.rule: UNT segment: first DE = segment count (including UNH and UNT); second DE = message reference matching UNH second DE; UNH+all_middle+UNT counted

valid_structure.UNT_format: UNT+{count}+{ref}' where count includes UNH and UNT themselves

edifact_orders.notes: If message has 4 segments (UNH, BGM, DTM, UNT), UNT first DE must be 4; reference in UNT must match UNH
